title: calamintha parviflora subsp. glandulosa
intro: Calamintha parviflora subsp. glandulosa, also known as small-leaved calamintha or mountain balm, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calamintha parviflora subsp. glandulosa include relief from digestive issues, reduced anxiety and stress, and improved respiratory function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
